workflow.name: Process multiple requests in FIFO using OpenAI Batch API and Supabase/Postgres
workflow.description:
# How it works
You provide a list of prompts and a system instruction, the workflow batches them into a single OpenAI Batch API request.
The batch job is tracked in a Supabase openai_batches table.
A cron job polls OpenAI every 5 minutes, and once the batch completes, the results are decoded and stored back in Supabase.
# Set up steps
Create the openai_batches table in Supabase. Schema is in the yellow sticky note.
Add your OpenAI and Supabase/Postgres credentials to the workflow.
Replace the mock data with your actual prompts and you're ready to go!

nodes.name: Call Batch API to retrieve batch object
Create a row in batch table

parameters.content:
# OpenAI Batch API (FIFO with Supabase / Postgres)

### How it works
This workflow uses OpenAI's **Batch API** to process multiple prompts at reduced cost, with Supabase as a persistent queue for tracking batch jobs.

**Phase 1 — Submit:** Takes an array of text inputs and a system prompt, converts them into `.jsonl` batch format, uploads to OpenAI's Files API, submits a batch job, and records it in the `openai_batches` table.

**Phase 2 — Poll & Retrieve (every 5 mins):** A cron job queries Supabase for the earliest uncompleted batch, checks its status via OpenAI, and either updates the status (if still processing) or downloads, decodes, and stores the completed results back into `openai_batches`.

### Setup steps
1. **Create the Supabase table** `openai_batches`:
   - `id` (text, PK) — OpenAI batch ID
   - `batch_status` (text, not null)
   - `output_file_id` (text, nullable)
   - `result` (text[], nullable) — array of LLM answers
   - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now())
   - `updated_at` (timestamptz, nullable)
2. Add your **OpenAI API credentials** to all HTTP Request nodes.
3. Add your **Supabase** and **Postgres** credentials.
4. Replace mock data in **Start** with your actual `systemPrompt` and `inputs` array.

### Customization
- Change the model in **Convert to batch requests** (currently `gpt-5-mini`).
- Adjust cron interval (currently every 5 mins).
- For strict FIFO, set the Postgres select to `LIMIT 1`.

## 📤 Phase 1: Submit Batch
Converts inputs to `.jsonl`, uploads to OpenAI, creates a batch job, and records it in `openai_batches`.

## 📥 Phase 2: Poll & Retrieve (cron)
Queries `openai_batches` for uncompleted batches, checks OpenAI status, downloads + decodes results and stores them back if completed.

Start (mock data).systemPrompt: You are a friendly assistant! Keep your responses concise and helpful. When asked to write, create, or help with something, just do it directly. Don't ask clarifying questions unless absolutely necessary - make reasonable assumptions and proceed with the task.

main.node: Create a row in batch table
Call Batch API to retrieve batch object

user.name: Teng Wei Herr
user.bio: Frontend engineer at Binance, building LLM applications on the side

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
